sentences.text: We're swooping back then.
Lester!
Hi, good morning, how are you?
Doing good, how are you?
I'm good, thank you.
How's the week starting off for ya?
A little rocky, but it's Tuesday.
Yeah.
I don't know, Tuesday's pretty early for it to already be rocky last year.
It's one day closer to Friday, you know?
You know what?
That's a good way to look at it.
I can appreciate that.
I had a busy weekend, so yesterday I was just like, oh my gosh, that's gonna be a long week, but it's already Tuesday, right?
I'm gonna go with your sense of it there.
Right.
So, how's everything going?
I know I saw your email, I obviously had sent you over the visual, shared an update.
Oh, I was… did I share an update with you yesterday?
I was planning to share an update with you yesterday, but we could talk through that, here today.
Yeah, I have an update for you, but kind of curious how your conversations are going, and any questions, concerns that you may have.
So, I, I spoke to Ken, Ken Hatfield, that's the person that's gonna be making the decision, so, He likes the idea of the automation.
Okay.
But we have, you know, some other stuff going on in the background, so right now he wants to hold off on this.
He didn't give me a timeline just yet.
But he knows that, you know, everything's pretty much being automated, so we're trying to catch up.
Okay.
So, because, yeah, because I know you guys aren't really using automation really fully yet.
So, sounds like that's on your radar, but… He's just not quite ready… What do you think is kind of holding him back?
Fair.
Can you hear me?
Buster?
I'm sorry, go ahead.
Okay, I was gonna say, what is it… what do you think is holding, kind of, holding him back from being open to, like, the… to moving forward with it?
Just the stuff that's going on the back end, so, on the background, rather.
So, we just implemented at least AI at one of the properties.
We have that going, of course, budget talks.
We have a few phone calls with rentables to, implement, I think it's the… automated, Facebook, Instagram.
Oh, social… were you talking to Nathan?
Yes, yes, yes, that, that one.
So there's a couple things going on on the background that…
You know, that are going on, and he's interested, for sure.
But we do… Just kind of want to wait and kind of go through the paperwork, see… You know, how is it gonna benefit?
Because he didn't see that just yet.
And then it's gonna lead us to make the decision of, okay, we're gonna go with this, and then we're gonna cut off the third party.
So, there's a lot of stuff to weigh in.
Gotcha.
Okay.
Is there anything, like, on my end that would be helpful while, like, you're making… having those conversations and making the decisions?
I can… you know.
Anything that would be helpful for you to present to him?
Not right now, with the last email you sent, I asked… Sent that to him, but he hasn't had a time to view it.
Okay.

The Elise AI integration has been kind of the issue right now, so we went live last Tuesday, and we're still trying to work out through everything and get, get familiar with that, and that's gonna take a little bit.
Sure.
Yeah, that makes sense.
I mean, I… at least AI, obviously… did you roll that out across all the properties?
No, it's just two, thankfully.
Okay, okay.

I think one of the things that I want to just… I could reiterate this in the email, is our… so I know with some of those softwares, like, the implementation process is a super heavy lift, and so, you know, whenever you're evaluating any kind of new tool, that's always usually, like, a concern, but… Just to kind of reiterate, it's very, very easy to set up our… product for you, so the social AI is literally just giving us access to your social pages via the backend of our rentable backend, so…
Right.
It's just one click there, and then our team sets up reputation with you.

You know, they do, like, a live walkthrough so that we can get it set up and automated exactly how you want, but… the social side of things, it's literally just, like, one click of a button in our backend, so there's no heavy lift from, like, an implementation standpoint, or, like, you know, training, or anything like that.
I think the beauty of the product, too, is, you know, especially for social, that it's fully automated, you really don't have… you don't really have to do anything once it's set up, right?
Right.
So, just something to keep in mind there that I can… I could certainly reiterate that.

And then, we are rolling out… I think I showed it to you on our on our last conversation, local posting, and that's gonna be live by the end of this month, and so we'll be posting local restaurants, in the area of that property, like, nice reputable restaurants, and highlighting, you know.
That they're close to your property, just to give a more localized feel to some of the content that's going out on social as well.
Oh, perfect.
So, automated local content's something that we had a lot of partners ask us for, and so we've been in beta.

I can send you a couple screenshots of what those posts would look like, but those would be included in the 8 automated posts a month, as well, but… I'm curious, do you think, like, toggling on a property for social, like, as a pilot would be something that Ken would do?
would potentially be interested in doing.

Like, we can turn one on for a few weeks, just so you guys, you know, at no cost, so you can get the feel of… of the automated social posts, you know, if that's something that maybe would help him kind of see the product in action and see how hands-off it is from, like, a implementation standpoint.
I think so, but let me get with him.
I, I'll follow up with him on, on the, last email I sent him, because he was supposed to go over, over the decks and, and just kind of get a feel from that.
But I don't think he's had a chance to do that.

Okay, well, I'll send you a couple screenshots of the local post, I'll send you, you know, what we could do from a pilot standpoint if you wanted to see, you know, if you wanted to just see it in action a few times.
And then, you know, do you want to try to follow up?
Do you want to schedule some time in a couple weeks to reconnect again, or you tell me?
Yeah, definitely.
Okay.
Let me see…
I should have something by… Early… the week of the 22nd.
Okay.
Do you want to do the same time on the… on the 23rd?
Sure, yeah, that works.
Would you… do you think that your… Ken would be interested in, like, hopping on and seeing, like, a live demo of the product?
I proposed that to him, and he told me not yet.
We're, hopping on a call with Nathan sometime this week, kind of same thing.
Kind of presented it, you know, presented it to him, and he's like, okay, yeah, let me see how that looks.
So we… We're hopping on a call this week.
Okay.
Yeah, I would say, you know, the products are super different.

One, obviously, Nathan's is much more focused on lead gen with, like, those targeted ads and the dash, the rentable dash, and whereas this is much more focused on that, creating that content engine for you, that automation, and then focusing it on your reputation.
So, really different, like.
kind of, I guess, Value adds for you.
But I would be happy to walk him through the reputation dashboard and show him the social posting as well, so if it's something that he's open to, we can add him to that meeting on the 23rd.
But I'll send you a follow-up, I'll include the offer to turn on a property at no cost for a few weeks from a social standpoint.
I'll show you those social posts, and then I just sent you the invite, and we can reconnect on the 23rd, and if he has any questions in between, just by all means, reach out to me.
Sounds good.
Alright, do you have any questions?
No?
Alright, thanks, Lester.
Appreciate your.
Thank you.
Alright, take care.
